codeReview.summary: The feature established the new auth surface and status command, but the reviewed commit did not fully satisfy the feature contract. In the reviewed implementation, `auth status` treated any non-empty environment token plus any present `TOGGL_API_URL` as an authenticated custom session without validating the URL, and its precedence messaging claimed a broader model than the runtime actually supports.

issues.description: `AuthStatusCommand::get_status()` reports an environment-backed custom session as authenticated whenever `TOGGL_API_TOKEN` is set and `TOGGL_API_URL` exists, but the reviewed commit never validates that URL. A malformed value like `TOGGL_API_URL=fake` is still surfaced as a healthy custom/OpenToggl session, which violates the requirement for actionable provider failures and explicit auth/provider status introspection.

The status output says `Credential Precedence: Environment (TOGGL_API_TOKEN) > Keychain > None`, but runtime provider selection also depends on `TOGGL_API_URL`, and environment mode completely overrides mutation semantics rather than simply being the first readable source. That wording is misleading relative to the actual supported model.

summary: Reviewed README, mission docs, feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, cli-worker skill, commit 66a6b2d, and relevant auth/credential code. Result: fail, because the reviewed auth status implementation reported malformed custom provider state as healthy and the precedence copy overstated the actual runtime model; these gaps were later corrected by follow-up fix work.
